text: Lecture	  1	  –	  Introduction	  &	  Limb	  Plan	   • Upper	  Limb	   o Arm	  –	  between	  shoulder	  and	  elbow	   o Forearm	  –	  between	  elbow	  and	  hand	   • Lower	  Limb	   o Thigh	  –	  between	  hip	  and	  knee	   o Leg	  –	  between	  knee	  and	  foot	   • Limbs	  are	  outgrowths	  of	  the	  body	  wall	  –	  no	  serous	  cavities	  etc.	  Just	  muscle,	  vessels	  and	  nerves.	   	   Developmental	  Origins	   • Trilaminar	  embryo	  	   o Ectoderm	   o Mesoderm	   o Endoderm	   • Within	  the	  mesoderm,	  adjacent	  to	  the	  neural	   tube	  is	  paraxial	  mesoderm	   • They	  give	  rise	  to	  somites,	  which	  are	  the	  segments	   of	  the	  body	   o Sclerotome	  (Scl)	  –	  bones	  of	  body	  segment	   o Dermomyotome	  (DM)	  	    Dermis	  of	  the	  body	  segment	  	    Skeletal	  muscle	  of	  the	  body	  segment	   • Dermatome	  and	  myotome	  migrate	  into	  the	  limbs,	  taking	  nerves	  and	  vessels	  with	  them.	   • Limb	  buds	   o Appear	  toward	  the	  end	  of	  the	  4th	  week	   o Arm	  buds	  appear	  a	  few	  days	  before	  the	  leg	  buds	   	   Segmentation	   • Neck	  –	  8	  	   • Thorax	  –	  12	   • Lumbar	  –	  5	   	   • Sacral	  –	  5	   • Coccygeal	  -­‐	  4	   • Each	  segment	  has	  its	  own	  nerve	  and	  with	  it	  a	  number.	   • Upper	  limb	  grow	  out	  from	  C5-­‐T1	   • Lower	  limb	  grows	  out	  from	  L2-­‐S3	   • The	  grow	  laterally	  and	  the	  rotate	  within	  the	  womb.	   	   Terminology	   • Developmental	  Position	   o Limbs	  outstretched,	  thumbs	  up	  as	  shown	  above.	   • Anatomical	  Position	   o Limbs	  by	  side,	  palms	  facing	  anteriorly	   • Functional	  Position	   o Limb	  by	  side,	  palms	  facing	  thighs	   	   Compartments	   • Flexor	  muscles,	  muscles	  that	  bring	  2	  developmentally	  ventral	  surfaces	  together	  are	  in	  the	  ventral	   compartment	   • Extensor	  muscles,	  muscles	  that	  bring	  2	  developmentally	  dorsal	  surfaces	  together	  are	  in	  the	  dorsal	   compartment.	   	   	   Limb Segments Upper limb : C5 – T1 Lower limb : L2 – S3 	   Bone	  plan	   • Girdle	   o Half	  ring	  that	  connects	  to	  axial	  skeleton	   • Single	  Bones	   o Arm	  and	  thigh	   • Two	  Bones	   o
